title: Chain-Link Rust
central_metaphor: A baseball backstop's chain-link fence, evolving from a literal playground boundary to a symbol of time's erosion and the permanent, bittersweet imprint of memory.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The old screen door’s got a crick in its swing
And the porch step groans a familiar tune
I can see the ghost of a chalk-drawn line
Fadin’ in the heat of a late June noon
[Pre-Chorus]
The air smells of cut grass and gasoline
From a mower that’s been gone ten years or more
[Chorus]
That old backstop’s still standin’ tall
But the chain-link’s got a brand-new rust
I can hear the crack, I can see the fall
In a cloud of forgotten dust
[Verse 2]
Found a bottle cap buried by the fence
Where we swore we’d hide a treasure map
Now the ink’s just blue on a sun-bleached page
And the X marks this, and I can’t go back
[Pre-Chorus]
The air smells of cut grass and gasoline
From a mower that’s been gone ten years or more
[Chorus]
That old backstop’s still standin’ tall
But the chain-link’s got a brand-new rust
I can hear the crack, I can see the fall
In a cloud of forgotten dust
[Bridge]
I drove by the new kid’s house today
He’s got a bike leaned up on that same old wall
I just kept on goin’ down the highway
‘Cause you can’t go home from a home-run call
[Final Chorus]
That old backstop’s still standin’ tall
But the chain-link’s got a brand-new rust
I can hear the crack, I can see the fall
In a cloud of dust that remembers us
